Include laboratory values associated with alkaline phosphatase (ALP) tests - Intestinal alkaline phosphatase measurement C0201853
Include laboratory values associated with alanine aminotransferase (ALT) tests - Alanine Aminotransferase Measurement C0201836

clinical_criteria: ALT >= 3x ULN AND Bilirubin >= 2x ULN (Bilirubin Upper Limit Normal = 40 umol/L or 2.34 mg/dL)
Exposure to drug within 3 months prior to liver injury diagnosis
Normal laboratory measurements prior to exposure to drug
Laboratory measurements reach threshold for DILI following drug administration
Peak ALT within 6mo after med >= 5x ULN
Peak ALP within 6mo after med >= 2x ULN
Peak ALT within 6mo after med >= 3x ULN
Peak Bilirubin within 6mo after med >= 2x ULN
Exclude if ALP >= 2x ULN within 1mo prior to med
Exclude if ALT >= 3x ULN within 1mo prior to med
Exclude if Bilirubin >= 2x ULN within 1mo prior to med
algorithm_summary: The DILI phenotype algorithm identifies inpatients with acute drug-induced liver injury through a multi-step process: 1) Identifies patients with liver injury diagnosis (using ICD-9 codes or NLP) AND drug exposure within 3 months prior to diagnosis, 2) Confirms acute (not chronic) liver injury, 3) Verifies laboratory thresholds are met (ALP >=2x ULN OR ALT >= 5x ULN OR (ALT>= 3xULN AND Bilirubin >= 2x ULN)), 4) Ensures temporal relationship between drug exposure and abnormal lab values, and 5) Excludes patients with other diagnoses that could explain liver injury (cancer, gallbladder disease, viral hepatitis, etc.). The algorithm uses both coded data and natural language processing to identify cases and includes specific exclusion criteria to minimize false positives.
